term: netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na me so attā
preferred_translation: this is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self
alternative_translations: this is not mine, this I am not, this is not my self
discouraged_translations: this does not belong to me, this is not who I really am, this is not the self
definition: Canonical three-part de-appropriation formula of not-mine, not-I-am, and not-self.
notes: Use this record when the text explicitly spells out the practical force of not-self. Keep the three turns distinct: no ownership, no I-am identification, and no self-claim. SN 22.59 anchors the formula in the practical not-self analysis of the heaps, and MN 148 uses it as the live pattern of identity-quenching.

example_phrases.pali: netaṁ mama, nesohamasmi, na me so attā
example_phrases.translation: this is not mine, I am not this, this is not my self
example_phrases.notes: Control line for the de-appropriation pattern in MN 148.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the practical de-appropriation formula that explains the force of not-self.
Supports the formula as the explicit path leading to the quenching of identity.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the practical not-self formula.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not turn the line into a metaphysical slogan and do not collapse its three movements into one abstract denial.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the de-appropriation formula from losing its ownership, identification, and self-claim structure.
